source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Postcards Collection
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Custom house, castle and jail, Sitka, Alaska
description: Historic gelatin silver postcard of the U.S. Custom House, the former Baranof Castle site (Castle Hill), and the territorial jail in Sitka, Alaska. Castle Hill is where the Alaska Transfer ceremony took place on October 18, 1867 — the formal handover of Russian America to the United States. The original Baranof Castle (Russian governor's residence) had burned in 1894; the buildings in this photograph are the American-era replacements on the same hilltop site.
alt: Historic sepia postcard of the U.S. Custom House, Castle Hill, and territorial jail in Sitka, Alaska
